2604.01202.title: Therefore I am. I Think
2604.01202.paper_summary: We consider the question: when a large language reasoning model makes a choice, did it think first and then decide to, or decide first and then think? In this paper, we present evidence that detectable, early-encoded decisions shape chain-of-thought in reasoning models. Specifically, we show that a simple linear probe successfully decodes tool-calling decisions from pre-generation activations with very high confidence, and in some cases, even before a single reasoning token is produced. Activation steering supports this causally: perturbing the decision direction leads to inflated deliberation, and flips behavior in many examples (between 7 - 79% depending on model and benchmark). We also show through behavioral analysis that, when steering changes the decision, the chain-of-thought process often rationalizes the flip rather than resisting it. Together, these results suggest that reasoning models can encode action choices before they begin to deliberate in text.
2604.01202.paper_summary_zh: 我們考慮這個問題：當大型語言推理模型做出選擇時，它是先思考然後決定，還是先決定然後思考？在本文中，我們提供證據表明，可檢測的、早期編碼的決策會影響推理模型的思考鏈。具體而言，我們展示了一個簡單的線性探針成功地從生成前的激活中以非常高的信心解碼工具調用決策，在某些情況下，甚至在產生單個推理標記之前。激活引導支持這一因果關係：擾動決策方向會導致延遲增加，並在許多例子中改變行為（根據模型和基準，變化範圍在7%到79%之間）。我們還通過行為分析顯示，當引導改變決策時，思考鏈過程往往會合理化這一改變，而不是抵抗它。綜合這些結果，這表明推理模型可以在開始文本推理之前編碼行動選擇。
2604.01202.author: Esakkivel Esakkiraja et.al.
2604.01202.authors: Esakkivel Esakkiraja, Sai Rajeswar, Denis Akhiyarov, Rajagopal Venkatesaramani
